 {"id":"rich_rust-ehe","title":"Create E2E Test: Table Rendering Scenarios","description":"## Objective
Comprehensive E2E tests for Table rendering with detailed logging.

## Background
Tables are the most complex renderable with many interacting features: column sizing, row rendering, borders, alignment. E2E tests ensure all features work together.

## Test Scenarios

### Scenario 1: Simple Table
```rust
#[test]
fn test_e2e_simple_table() {
    let _guard = init_test_logging();
    let span = info_span!("e2e_simple_table");
    let _enter = span.enter();
    
    let table = Table::new()
        .add_column(Column::new("Name"))
        .add_column(Column::new("Age"))
        .add_row(Row::new(vec![Cell::new("Alice"), Cell::new("30")]))
        .add_row(Row::new(vec![Cell::new("Bob"), Cell::new("25")]));
    
    debug!(columns = 2, rows = 2, "Table structure");
    
    let console = Console::new().width(40);
    let output = console.render_to_string(&table);
    
    debug!(output = %output, "Rendered table");
    
    // Verify structure
    assert!(output.contains("Name"), "Missing header");
    assert!(output.contains("Alice"), "Missing data");
    assert!(output.lines().count() >= 4, "Insufficient lines");
    
    info!(lines = output.lines().count(), "Table rendered successfully");
}
```

### Scenario 2: Table with All Features
- Title and caption
- Header and footer
- Multiple box styles
- Styled cells
- Vertical alignment in cells

### Scenario 3: Width Constraints
- Table wider than terminal (must collapse)
- Table narrower than terminal (must expand)
- Columns at minimum width
- Ratio-based column distribution

### Scenario 4: Wide Characters in Cells
- CJK characters
- Emoji
- Mixed ASCII/CJK

### Scenario 5: Multi-line Cells
- Cells with newlines
- Cells that wrap
- Vertical alignment with multi-line

### Scenario 6: Edge Cases
- Empty table
- Single cell
- Very narrow width (force minimum)
- Very wide content

## Logging Requirements
Each test must log:
1. **Setup**: Table structure (columns, rows, options)
2. **Width calculation**: Input widths, available width, result widths
3. **Row rendering**: Cell content, alignment applied
4. **Border drawing**: Box style, characters used
5. **Final output**: Complete rendered string
6. **Timing**: Time for width calc, time for render

## Golden Files
Create golden files for each scenario to detect visual regressions.

## Files to Create
- tests/e2e_table.rs
- tests/golden/table_*.txt","acceptance_criteria":"All table scenarios pass. Width algorithms verified. Logs show calculation details.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:44:29.700982578-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T12:37:41.691805306-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T12:37:41.691805306-05:00","close_reason":"Completed 37 Table E2E tests covering all scenarios: simple tables, full features, width constraints, wide characters, alignment, edge cases, and snapshot tests","labels":["e2e","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-ehe","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-6d6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:45:12.880043097-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"rich_rust-ehe","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-4ot","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T11:11:54.007910488-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}

 {"id":"rich_rust-g92","title":"Validate Color System against RICH_SPEC Section 1","description":"# Validate Color System against RICH_SPEC Section 1

## Context
The Color System is foundational to rich_rust. It handles color representation, parsing, palette management, and ANSI code generation.

## Spec Requirements to Validate (from RICH_SPEC.md §1)

### 1.1 Data Structures
- [ ] ColorTriplet: `hex()`, `rgb()`, `normalized()` methods
- [ ] ColorSystem enum: STANDARD (1), EIGHT_BIT (2), TRUECOLOR (3), WINDOWS (4)
- [ ] ColorType enum: DEFAULT (0), STANDARD (1), EIGHT_BIT (2), TRUECOLOR (3), WINDOWS (4)
- [ ] Color struct: name, color_type, number, triplet fields

### 1.2 Color Parsing (CRITICAL)
- [ ] Named colors: "red", "bright_blue" → STANDARD/EIGHT_BIT
- [ ] Hex format: "#FF0000" → TRUECOLOR
- [ ] Color number: "color(196)" → STANDARD if 0-15, EIGHT_BIT if 16-255
- [ ] RGB format: "rgb(255,0,0)" → TRUECOLOR
- [ ] Default: "default" → ColorType::DEFAULT
- [ ] Case-insensitivity
- [ ] Whitespace handling in rgb()
- [ ] **LRU cache (max 1024 entries)** - verify caching exists

### 1.3 Color Palettes
- [ ] STANDARD_PALETTE: 16 colors with exact RGB values
- [ ] EIGHT_BIT_PALETTE: 256 colors (16 standard + 216 cube + 24 grayscale)
- [ ] WINDOWS_PALETTE: 16 colors with exact RGB values
- [ ] 6x6x6 cube formula: `16 + 36 * red_index + 6 * green_index + blue_index`
- [ ] Grayscale ramp: indices 232-255 with values (8,8,8) to (238,238,238)

### 1.4 Color Conversion Algorithms
- [ ] RGB to 8-bit: grayscale detection via HLS saturation < 0.15
- [ ] RGB to Standard: weighted CIE76 color distance formula
- [ ] Verify exact formulas match spec

### 1.5 ANSI Code Generation
- [ ] DEFAULT: ["39"] foreground, ["49"] background
- [ ] STANDARD 0-7: ["30"+n] fg, ["40"+n] bg
- [ ] STANDARD 8-15: ["82"+n] fg, ["92"+n] bg
- [ ] EIGHT_BIT: ["38", "5", "N"] fg, ["48", "5", "N"] bg
- [ ] TRUECOLOR: ["38", "2", "R", "G", "B"] fg, ["48", "2", "R", "G", "B"] bg

## Files to Check
- `src/color.rs` (955 lines)

## Validation Approach
1. Read src/color.rs thoroughly
2. Compare each implementation detail against spec
3. Create tests for any uncovered edge cases
4. Document discrepancies
5. Fix implementation or update spec as needed

## Acceptance Criteria
- All spec requirements validated with tests
- LRU caching verified or implemented
- Color conversion algorithms match exactly
- ANSI code generation matches spec table","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:13:47.105219826-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T23:34:03.392808606-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T23:34:03.392811762-05:00","close_reason":"Color System validation complete - all RICH_SPEC Section 1 requirements verified:
- ColorTriplet, ColorSystem, ColorType, Color data structures match spec
- Color parsing (named, hex, rgb, color(N), default) implemented with LRU cache (1024 entries)
- STANDARD/WINDOWS/EIGHT_BIT palettes have correct RGB values
- 6x6x6 color cube formula: 16 + 36*r + 6*g + b verified
- Grayscale ramp (232-255) with values (8,8,8) to (238,238,238) verified
- rgb_to_eight_bit uses HLS saturation < 0.15 for grayscale detection
- ANSI code generation matches spec for all color types","labels":["phase-1","validation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-g92","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-q1k","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:19:35.645784979-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"rich_rust-gk4","title":"collapse_widths() missing rounding error correction loop","description":"## Minor Discrepancy Found

**Location**: src/renderables/table.rs:574-610

**Spec Requirement (RICH_SPEC Section 9.3, lines 1680-1694)**:
After proportional shrinking, there should be an explicit rounding error correction loop that removes any remaining excess from the largest shrinkable column.

**Actual Behavior**:
The implementation uses inline `remaining_excess` tracking but does not have the post-loop correction from the spec.

**Impact**:
Minor - may result in off-by-one differences in edge cases where integer division rounding doesn't perfectly distribute the excess.

**Validation Test**:
See tests/e2e_width_algorithms.rs

**Recommended Fix**:
Add the rounding correction loop from spec lines 1680-1694 after the main proportional shrink loop.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T13:14:32.977555554-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T23:32:06.073256642-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T23:32:06.073256642-05:00","close_reason":"Fixed: Added rounding error correction loop per RICH_SPEC Section 9.3 (lines 1680-1694). After proportional shrinking, implementation now checks if new_total > available and removes excess from columns in reverse order. Tests pass (test_collapse_widths_proportional_shrink). Implementation at table.rs:680-695.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-gk4","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-q1k","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T13:14:39.089609282-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}

Supports themes, line numbers, file loading, indent guides."}

 {"id":"rich_rust-q5f","title":"Validate Table Width Algorithms against RICH_SPEC Section 9.4","description":"## Objective
Verify Table width calculation algorithms match RICH_SPEC.md Section 9.4 EXACTLY.

## Background
This is the CRITICAL algorithm validation for Table. Width calculation is complex and must match spec precisely to avoid layout bugs.

## Validation Checklist

### get_row_widths()
- [ ] Measures each cell in row
- [ ] Returns Vec<Measurement> with min/max per column
- [ ] Accounts for padding
- [ ] Handles multi-line cells correctly

### calculate_widths() Main Algorithm
- [ ] Step 1: Get minimum and maximum widths per column
- [ ] Step 2: Calculate total minimum width
- [ ] Step 3: If total minimum > available: use minimums (clipped)
- [ ] Step 4: If total maximum <= available AND expand: use maximums
- [ ] Step 5: If total minimum < available < total maximum: distribute
- [ ] Step 6: Account for borders and padding in available width

### expand_widths() - Distributing Extra Space
- [ ] Calculate extra = available - current_total
- [ ] Distribute by ratio (ratio_distribute algorithm)
- [ ] Columns without ratio get default ratio = 1
- [ ] Columns with ratio = 0 get no extra space
- [ ] Verify: sum of distributed = extra exactly (no rounding loss)

### collapse_widths() - Reducing Widths
- [ ] Calculate excess = current_total - available
- [ ] Calculate reducible space per column = current - minimum
- [ ] Distribute reduction proportionally to reducible space
- [ ] Never go below minimum
- [ ] When column hits minimum, redistribute to others
- [ ] Verify: sum of reductions = excess exactly

## Test Vectors (from spec)
```
Test 1: expand_widths
  Input:  widths=[20,20,20], ratios=[1,2,1], available=100
  Expect: [30,40,30]  (extra 40 distributed 1:2:1)

Test 2: collapse_widths
  Input:  widths=[40,30,30], minimums=[10,10,10], available=70
  Expect: ~[27,21,22] (excess 30 reduced proportionally)

Test 3: boundary
  Input:  widths=[10,10,10], minimums=[10,10,10], available=20
  Expect: [10,10,10] (can't go below minimum, clipped)
```

## Logging Requirements
When running tests, log:
- Input widths, minimums, maximums
- Available width
- Each step of the algorithm
- Final output widths
- Verification that sum matches

## Files to Examine
- src/renderables/table.rs (width calculation functions)
- RICH_SPEC.md Section 9.4","acceptance_criteria":"Width algorithms match spec EXACTLY. All test vectors pass. Logs show calculation steps.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:46:41.664745764-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T13:14:47.104456048-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T13:14:47.104456048-05:00","close_reason":"## Validation Complete

### Findings Summary

1. **calculate_widths()** - CORRECT: Main algorithm follows spec structure

2. **expand_widths()** - CRITICAL DISCREPANCY (rich_rust-t54):
   - Distributes extra space equally instead of by column ratio
   - Column.ratio field is completely ignored
   
3. **collapse_widths()** - MINOR DISCREPANCY (rich_rust-gk4):
   - Missing rounding error correction loop from spec
   - May cause off-by-one in edge cases

### Deliverables
- Created tests/e2e_width_algorithms.rs with test vectors
- Created bug issues: rich_rust-t54 (P1), rich_rust-gk4 (P2)
- All tests pass

### Test Vector Evidence
- expand_widths: widths=[20,20,20], ratios=[1,2,1], available=100
  - Expected: [30,40,30], Actual: [33,33,34]","labels":["phase-1","validation"]}
 {"id":"rich_rust-qgs","title":"Validate Markup Parser against RICH_SPEC Section 4","description":"## Objective
Verify the Markup parser in src/markup/mod.rs matches RICH_SPEC.md Section 4 exactly.

## Background
The markup parser converts Rich-style markup like '[bold red]Hello[/]' into styled segments. It's the primary user-facing API for styling text. The parser handles nested tags, escaping, and error recovery.

## Validation Checklist

### 4.1 Tag Syntax
- [ ] Verify opening tag: [style] where style is parsed by Style::parse
- [ ] Verify closing tag: [/] closes most recent tag
- [ ] Verify named close: [/bold] closes specific tag
- [ ] Verify escape sequence: backslash-bracket produces literal bracket

### 4.2 ParseElement Enum
- [ ] Verify variants: Text(String), Open(Tag), Close(Option<String>)
- [ ] Verify Tag struct contains: name (String), style (Style)

### 4.3 Parser Behavior
- [ ] Verify render(markup) returns Vec<Segment>
- [ ] Verify nested tags: [bold][red]text[/][/] applies both styles
- [ ] Verify style inheritance: inner tags combine with outer
- [ ] Verify unmatched close tags are treated as literal text
- [ ] Verify unclosed tags at end are auto-closed

### 4.4 Error Handling (MarkupError)
- [ ] Verify InvalidTag for malformed tags
- [ ] Verify UnmatchedClose for close without open
- [ ] Verify error recovery: invalid markup rendered as literal

### 4.5 Complex Cases
- [ ] Test: [bold]a[italic]b[/italic]c[/bold] - proper nesting
- [ ] Test: [red]a[blue]b[/]c[/] - color override then restore
- [ ] Test: [link=URL]click[/] - hyperlink
- [ ] Test: escaped brackets - literal brackets
- [ ] Test: [bold red on blue]text[/] - compound style

## Files to Examine
- src/markup/mod.rs (primary)
- src/style.rs (Style::parse)
- RICH_SPEC.md Section 4","acceptance_criteria":"All checklist items verified. Parser handles all edge cases correctly.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:17:05.871978582-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T23:35:40.042934881-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T23:35:40.042934881-05:00","close_reason":"Markup Parser validation complete - all RICH_SPEC Section 4 requirements verified:
- Tag syntax: [tag], [/], [/tag], [tag=param], [@handler] supported
- Regex pattern: (\\*)\[([a-z#/@][^\[\]]*?)\] matches spec (improved to exclude both brackets)
- Parsing algorithm: optimization for no '[', style stack, escape handling, auto-close unclosed tags
- Error handling: MarkupError with UnmatchedClosingTag and InvalidTag variants
- 17 unit tests covering: plain text, bold, nesting, multiple styles, escaping, unclosed tags, link tags, colors
- render(), render_or_plain(), escape() functions all match spec","labels":["phase-1","validation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-qgs","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-q1k","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:19:35.801842899-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"rich_rust-r5g","title":"Fix golden_columns_multiline test: trailing whitespace difference","description":"The golden_columns_multiline test is failing due to a trailing whitespace difference:

- 'Second column\nalso multiline'

 {"id":"rich_rust-shw","title":"Validate Console against RICH_SPEC Section 7","description":"## Objective
Verify the Console implementation in src/console.rs matches RICH_SPEC.md Section 7 exactly.

## Background
Console is the central entry point for rich_rust. It detects terminal capabilities, manages output, and coordinates the rendering pipeline from markup to ANSI codes.

### 7.1 ConsoleOptions Struct
- [ ] Verify fields: max_width, color_system, force_terminal, record, highlight, emoji, safe_box, soft_wrap
- [ ] Verify immutable pattern: options passed to renderables
- [ ] Verify defaults match spec

### 7.2 ConsoleDimensions
- [ ] Verify fields: width, height
- [ ] Verify terminal size detection (fallback to 80x25)
- [ ] Verify COLUMNS/LINES env var override

### 7.3 Console Struct
- [ ] Verify builder pattern: Console::new().width(100).color_system(...)
- [ ] Verify terminal detection: is_terminal(), is_dumb_terminal()
- [ ] Verify color system auto-detection: COLORTERM, TERM env vars
- [ ] Verify force_terminal option bypasses detection

### 7.4 Console Methods

- [ ] Verify print_styled(text, style) convenience method
- [ ] Verify rule(title) prints horizontal rule
- [ ] Verify clear() clears screen
- [ ] Verify size() returns ConsoleDimensions

### 7.5 Rendering Pipeline
- [ ] Verify: markup parsing -> segment generation -> ANSI encoding
- [ ] Verify style application respects color_system
- [ ] Verify newlines are handled correctly
- [ ] Verify control segments produce correct escape codes

### 7.6 Recording (if implemented)
- [ ] Verify record=true captures output
- [ ] Verify export_text() returns plain text
- [ ] Verify export_html() returns HTML (if implemented)

## Files to Examine
- src/console.rs (primary)
- src/terminal.rs (terminal detection)
- RICH_SPEC.md Section 7","acceptance_criteria":"All checklist items verified.  {"id":"rich_rust-t54","title":"expand_widths() ignores column ratios, distributes space equally","description":"## Discrepancy Found

**Location**: src/renderables/table.rs:612-637

**Spec Requirement (RICH_SPEC Section 14.4)**:
Distribute extra space among columns proportionally based on their `ratio` field. Columns with ratio=0 get no extra space.

**Actual Behavior**:
The implementation distributes extra space EQUALLY across all columns, completely ignoring the `ratio` field.

**Test Vector**:
- Input: widths=[20,20,20], ratios=[1,2,1], available=100
- Expected: [30,40,30] (extra 40 distributed 1:2:1)
- Actual: [33,33,34] (extra 40 distributed equally)

**Impact**:
The Column.ratio(n) builder method exists and sets the field, but it has no effect on table layout during expansion.

**Validation Test**:
See tests/e2e_width_algorithms.rs

**Recommended Fix**:
Rewrite expand_widths() to follow Section 14.4 ratio_distribute algorithm.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T13:14:24.140591733-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T23:31:20.880305518-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T23:31:20.880305518-05:00","close_reason":"Verified fixed: expand_widths() now correctly uses num_rational::Ratio for ratio-based distribution. All e2e_width_algorithms tests pass (13/13).  {"id":"rich_rust-tkj","title":"Set Up CI/CD Pipeline with Test Artifacts","description":"## Objective
Create CI/CD pipeline that runs all tests and preserves artifacts for debugging.

## Background
CI is essential for catching regressions before merge. The pipeline must run unit tests, E2E tests, and benchmarks, preserving logs and artifacts for debugging failures.

## Implementation Requirements

### GitHub Actions Workflow (.github/workflows/ci.yml)
```yaml
name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [main, master]
  pull_request:

env:
  CARGO_TERM_COLOR: always
  RUST_LOG: debug
  RUST_BACKTRACE: 1

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Install Rust
        uses: dtolnay/rust-action@stable
        
      - name: Cache cargo
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: |
            ~/.cargo/registry
            ~/.cargo/git
            target
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-cargo-${{ hashFiles('**/Cargo.lock') }}
          
      - name: Run unit tests
        run: cargo test --all-features -- --nocapture 2>&1 | tee test-output.log
        
      - name: Run E2E tests
        run: cargo test --test '*' -- --nocapture 2>&1 | tee e2e-output.log
        
      - name: Upload test logs
        if: always()
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: test-logs
          path: |
            test-output.log
            e2e-output.log
          retention-days: 30
          
  benchmarks:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Run benchmarks
        run: cargo bench -- --noplot 2>&1 | tee bench-output.log
        
      - name: Upload benchmark results
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: benchmark-results
          path: |
            bench-output.log
            target/criterion
          retention-days: 90
          
  coverage:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Install cargo-tarpaulin
        run: cargo install cargo-tarpaulin
        
      - name: Generate coverage
        run: cargo tarpaulin --out Xml --output-dir coverage
        
      - name: Upload coverage
        uses: codecov/codecov-action@v4
        with:
          files: coverage/cobertura.xml
```

### Artifact Preservation
- Test output logs (30 days)
- Benchmark results (90 days)
- Coverage reports
- Golden file diffs on failure

### Failure Notifications
- GitHub status checks
- Optional: Slack/email on failure

## Files to Create
- .github/workflows/ci.yml
- .github/workflows/benchmarks.yml (optional separate)","acceptance_criteria":"CI runs on every PR. Artifacts preserved. Coverage tracked.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:47:45.408661933-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T01:31:53.710507013-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-18T01:31:53.710507013-05:00","close_reason":"Enhanced CI/CD pipeline with test artifacts, benchmarks, and coverage jobs","labels":["ci","infrastructure"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-tkj","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-6d6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:48:04.393233497-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
